title: ContainerDefinition
description: A container definition describes a container within a task definition.

name.description: The name of a container. Up to 255 characters (uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens).

image.description: The image used to start a container. Images in the Docker Hub registry are available by default. You can also specify an ECR repository.

cpu.description: The number of cpu units reserved for the container.

memory.description: The amount (in MiB) of memory to present to the container. Corresponds to the hard limit.

memoryReservation.description: The soft limit (in MiB) of memory to reserve for the container.

links.description: Allows containers to communicate without port mappings. Only supported for bridge network mode.

portMappings.description: The list of port mappings for the container.

essential.description: If the essential parameter of a container is marked as true and that container fails or stops, all other containers that are part of the task are stopped.

entryPoint.description: The entry point that is passed to the container.

command.description: The command that is passed to the container.

environment.description: The environment variables to pass to a container.

environmentFiles.description: A list of files containing the environment variables to pass to a container.

mountPoints.description: The mount points for data volumes in your container.

volumesFrom.description: Data volumes to mount from another container.

secrets.description: The secrets to pass to the container from AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store.

dependsOn.description: The dependencies defined for container startup and shutdown.

startTimeout.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before giving up on resolving dependencies for a container.

stopTimeout.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before the container is forcefully killed if it does not exit normally on its own.

user.description: The user to run as inside the container.

workingDirectory.description: The working directory to run commands inside the container in.

privileged.description: When this parameter is true, the container is given elevated privileges on the host container instance.

readonlyRootFilesystem.description: When this parameter is true, the container is given read-only access to its root file system.

dockerLabels.description: A key/value map of labels to add to the container.
